"And now, my brothers, what person in their natural state knows these things? I say to you, no one knows these things except the repentant."

"The person who repents, exercises faith, does good works, and prays continually without stopping—to such a person it is given to know the mysteries of God. To such a person it will be given to reveal things that have never been revealed before. To such a person it will be given to bring thousands of souls to repentance, just as it has been given to us to bring these our brothers to repentance."
"Do you remember, my brothers, that we told our people in the land of Zarahemla that we were going to the land of Nephi to preach to the Lamanites, and they laughed at us?"

"For they said to us, 'Do you think you can bring the Lamanites to the knowledge of the truth? Do you think you can convince the Lamanites that the traditions of their fathers are wrong—such a stubborn people whose hearts delight in bloodshed, whose days have been spent in the worst kinds of sin, whose ways have been the ways of wrongdoers from the beginning?' My brothers, you remember that this was what they said."
"They also said, 'Let us take up weapons against them and destroy them and their wickedness from the land, before they overrun us and destroy us.'"
"But, my beloved brothers, we came into the wilderness not with the intention to destroy our brothers, but with the hope that perhaps we might save a few of their souls."

"When our hearts were discouraged and we were about to turn back, the Lord comforted us and said, 'Go among your brothers, the Lamanites, and bear your afflictions patiently, and I will give you success.'"

"And we have come and gone among them. We have been patient in our sufferings and have endured every hardship. We have traveled from house to house, relying not on the mercies of the world alone, but on the mercies of God."

"We have entered their homes and taught them. We have taught them in their streets. We have taught them on their hills. We have entered their temples and synagogues and taught them. We have been cast out, mocked, spit upon, and struck on the face. We have been stoned, tied up with strong ropes, and thrown into prison. But through the power and wisdom of God, we have been set free again."
"We have suffered all kinds of hardships, all for the chance that we might save some soul. We believed our joy would be complete if we could be the means of saving some."

"Now we can look and see the fruits of our labors. Are they few? I say to you, no, they are many! We can see the sincerity of these converts because of their love toward their brothers and also toward us."
"For they would rather give up their lives than take the life of even an enemy. They have buried their weapons of war deep in the earth because of their love for their brothers."
"And now I ask you, has there ever been such great love in all the land? I say to you, no, there has not—not even among the Nephites."

"For the Nephites would take up weapons against their brothers and would not let themselves be killed. But look at how many of these converts have laid down their lives! And we know they have gone to their God because of their love and their hatred of sin."

"Do we not have reason to rejoice? I say to you, there have never been people who had so great a reason to rejoice as we do, since the world began. My joy overflows, even to boasting in my God, for he has all power, all wisdom, and all understanding. He comprehends all things, and he is a merciful Being, even to the point of salvation, for those who will repent and believe on his name."

"Now if this is boasting, then I will boast! For this is my life and my light, my joy and my salvation, and my redemption from everlasting sorrow. Blessed is the name of my God, who has remembered this people—a branch of the tree of Israel that was lost from the main body in a strange land. I say, blessed be the name of my God, who has remembered us, wanderers in a strange land."

"Now, my brothers, we see that God is mindful of every people, in whatever land they may be. He counts his people, and his compassion extends over all the earth. This is my joy and my great thanksgiving, and I will give thanks to my God forever. Amen."

27.text: After those Lamanites who had gone to war against the Nephites found, after many battles, that it was pointless to try to destroy them, they returned to the land of Nephi.

The Amalekites, because of their losses, were extremely angry. When they saw they could not get revenge against the Nephites, they began to stir up the people in anger against their brothers, the people of Anti-Nephi-Lehi. They began to destroy them again.
But this people again refused to take up weapons, and they allowed themselves to be killed according to the desires of their enemies.

When Ammon and his brothers saw this destruction among those they loved so dearly—people who had loved them in return, treating them as if they were angels sent from God to save them from everlasting destruction—when Ammon and his brothers saw this great destruction, they were moved with compassion and said to the king:
"Let us gather these people of the Lord together and go down to the land of Zarahemla, to our brothers the Nephites. Let us flee from our enemies so we will not be destroyed."
But the king said to them, "The Nephites will destroy us because of the many murders and sins we have committed against them."
Ammon said, "I will go and ask the Lord, and if he tells us to go down to our brothers, will you go?"
The king said to him, "Yes, if the Lord tells us to go, we will go down to our brothers and be their slaves until we have made up for the many murders and sins we have committed against them."
But Ammon said, "It is against the law of our people, established by my father, to have any slaves among them. So let us go down and rely on the mercy of our brothers."
But the king said, "Ask the Lord, and if he tells us to go, we will go. Otherwise we will die in this land."
Ammon went and asked the Lord, and the Lord said to him:

"Get this people out of this land so they do not perish. Satan has a strong hold on the hearts of the Amalekites, who stir up the Lamanites to anger against their brothers to kill them. Get out of this land. Blessed are this people in this generation, for I will preserve them."
Ammon went and told the king everything the Lord had said to him.

They gathered together all their people—all the people of the Lord—along with all their flocks and herds, and left the land. They traveled into the wilderness that divided the land of Nephi from the land of Zarahemla and came near the borders of the land.
Ammon said to them, "My brothers and I will go into the land of Zarahemla. You stay here until we return. We will test the hearts of our brothers to see if they will welcome you into their land."
As Ammon was traveling into the land, he and his brothers met Alma at the place we have already mentioned. This was a joyful meeting.
Ammon's joy was so great that he was completely filled with it. He was so overwhelmed by the joy of his God that his strength gave out, and he fell to the earth.
Was this not the greatest of joy? This is the kind of joy that only the truly repentant and humble seeker of happiness receives.
Alma's joy at meeting his brothers was also truly great, as was the joy of Aaron, Omner, and Himni. But their joy did not overcome their physical strength.
Alma led his brothers back to the land of Zarahemla, even to his own house. They went and told the chief judge everything that had happened to them in the land of Nephi among the Lamanites.
The chief judge sent a proclamation throughout all the land, asking the people to vote on whether to admit their brothers, the people of Anti-Nephi-Lehi.

The voice of the people came back, saying, "We will give up the land of Jershon, which is on the east by the sea, next to the land of Bountiful, which is south of the land of Bountiful. This land of Jershon is the land we will give to our brothers as an inheritance."

"And we will station our armies between the land of Jershon and the land of Nephi to protect our brothers in the land of Jershon. We do this for our brothers because they are afraid to take up weapons against their brothers for fear of committing sin. This great fear came from the deep repentance they had because of their many murders and their terrible wickedness."

"This is what we will do for our brothers: they will inherit the land of Jershon, and we will guard them from their enemies with our armies, on the condition that they give us a portion of what they have to help us support our armies."

When Ammon heard this, he returned to the people of Anti-Nephi-Lehi, and Alma went with him, into the wilderness where they had set up their tents. He told them everything. Alma also shared with them the story of his own conversion, along with the experiences of Ammon, Aaron, and his brothers.
This caused great joy among them. They went down into the land of Jershon and took possession of it. The Nephites called them the people of Ammon, and they were known by that name from then on.

They lived among the people of Nephi and were counted as members of the church of God. They were known for their devotion to God and also to other people. They were perfectly honest and upright in all things, and they were firm in the faith of Christ to the very end.

They looked upon shedding the blood of their brothers with the deepest horror. They could never be convinced to take up weapons against their brothers. They never looked upon death with any degree of fear because of their hope and faith in Christ and the resurrection. For them, death was swallowed up by the victory of Christ over it.
They would suffer death in the most painful and distressing ways their brothers could inflict before they would take a sword or weapon to strike them.
And so they were a devoted and beloved people, a highly favored people of the Lord.

28.text: After the people of Ammon were settled in the land of Jershon, a church was established in the land of Jershon, and the armies of the Nephites were stationed all around the land of Jershon—in all the borders around the land of Zarahemla—the armies of the Lamanites followed their brothers into the wilderness.
There was a tremendous battle—one like had never been known among all the people in the land since the time Lehi left Jerusalem. Tens of thousands of the Lamanites were killed and scattered.
There was also a tremendous slaughter among the people of Nephi. Nevertheless, the Lamanites were driven back and scattered, and the people of Nephi returned to their land.
This was a time when great mourning and sorrow was heard throughout all the land, among all the people of Nephi—

The cries of widows mourning for their husbands, fathers mourning for their sons, daughters mourning for their brothers, and brothers mourning for their fathers. The sound of mourning was heard among all of them, grieving for their family members who had been killed.
Surely this was a sorrowful day—a time of deep seriousness and a time of much fasting and prayer.
And so ended the fifteenth year of the reign of the judges over the people of Nephi.

This is the account of Ammon and his brothers—their travels in the land of Nephi, their sufferings in the land, their sorrows and afflictions, their incredible joy, and the welcome and safety of their brothers in the land of Jershon. May the Lord, the Redeemer of all people, bless their souls forever.
This is the account of the wars and conflicts among the Nephites, and also the wars between the Nephites and the Lamanites. The fifteenth year of the reign of the judges has ended.
From the first year to the fifteenth, many thousands of lives were destroyed. It brought about a terrible scene of bloodshed.

The bodies of many thousands lie buried in the earth, while the bodies of many thousands decay in heaps on the surface of the earth. Many thousands mourn the loss of their loved ones because they have reason to fear, according to the promises of the Lord, that their loved ones are in a state of endless misery.

While many thousands of others also mourn the loss of their loved ones, they rejoice and find hope—and even know, according to the promises of the Lord—that their loved ones have been raised to dwell at the right hand of God in a state of never-ending happiness.
And so we see how great the inequality among people is because of sin, transgression, and the power of the devil, which comes through the cunning plans he has created to trap the hearts of men.

And so we see the great need for people to work diligently in the vineyards of the Lord. We see the great reason for both sorrow and rejoicing—sorrow because of death and destruction among people, and joy because of the light of Christ leading to life.

29.text: Oh, if only I were an angel and could have the wish of my heart! Then I would go out and speak with the trumpet of God, with a voice to shake the earth, and cry repentance to every people!

I would declare to every soul, as with the voice of thunder, repentance and the plan of redemption—that they should repent and come to our God so there would be no more sorrow upon all the face of the earth.
But I am only a man, and I sin in my wish. I should be content with the things the Lord has given me to do.

I should not challenge the firm decree of a just God with my desires, for I know that he grants to people according to their desires, whether it leads to death or to life. I know that he assigns to people decrees that cannot be changed, based on their own choices, whether those choices lead to salvation or to destruction.

I know that both good and evil have been placed before all people. Those who do not know good from evil are blameless. But those who know good from evil are given consequences according to their desires, whether they desire good or evil, life or death, joy or a guilty conscience.
Now since I know these things, why should I want anything more than to do the work I have been called to do?
Why should I wish I were an angel, so that I could speak to every corner of the earth?

For the Lord sends teachers to every nation, from among their own people and in their own language, to teach his word. In his wisdom, he gives them everything he sees fit for them to have. So we see that the Lord gives guidance in wisdom, according to what is just and true.

I know what the Lord has commanded me, and I take joy in it. I do not take pride in myself, but I take pride in what the Lord has commanded me. This is my glory—that perhaps I may be an instrument in the hands of God to bring some soul to repentance. This is my joy.

When I see many of my brothers truly repentant and coming to the Lord their God, my soul is filled with joy. Then I remember what the Lord has done for me—that he heard my prayer. Then I remember his merciful arm that he reached out toward me.

I also remember the captivity of my ancestors, for I know for certain that the Lord delivered them from bondage, and by doing this he established his church. The Lord God—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob—delivered them from bondage.
I have always remembered the captivity of my ancestors. That same God who delivered them from the hands of the Egyptians also delivered them from bondage.
That same God established his church among them. That same God has called me through a holy calling to preach the word to this people and has given me much success, which fills me with joy.
But I do not rejoice only in my own success. My joy is even greater because of the success of my brothers who went to the land of Nephi.
They have worked extremely hard and have produced much fruit. How great their reward will be!
When I think about the success of my brothers, my soul is carried away—almost to the point of separating from my body—so great is my joy.

And now may God grant that my brothers may sit down in the kingdom of God, along with all those who are the fruit of their labors, that they may never leave, but that they may praise him forever. And may God grant that it may be done according to my words, just as I have spoken. Amen.

30.text: Now after the people of Ammon were settled in the land of Jershon, and after the Lamanites had been driven out of the land and their dead had been buried by the people—

The dead could not be counted because there were so many of them, and the Nephite dead were not counted either. But after they had buried their dead, and after the days of fasting, mourning, and prayer (this was in the sixteenth year of the reign of the judges over the people of Nephi), there began to be lasting peace throughout all the land.

Yes, and the people were careful to keep the commandments of the Lord. They were strict in observing the ordinances of God according to the law of Moses, because they had been taught to keep the law of Moses until it would be fulfilled.
And so the people had no trouble at all during the sixteenth year of the reign of the judges over the people of Nephi.
And it happened that at the beginning of the seventeenth year of the reign of the judges, there was continued peace.

But toward the end of the seventeenth year, a man came into the land of Zarahemla, and he was Anti-Christ, because he began to preach to the people against the prophecies that the prophets had spoken about the coming of Christ.
Now there was no law against what a person believed, because it was strictly against the commands of God to have a law that would put people on unequal ground.
For the scripture says: Choose today whom you will serve.
Now if a person wanted to serve God, that was his right. Or rather, if he believed in God, it was his right to serve Him. But if he did not believe in God, there was no law to punish him.

But if he murdered, he was punished with death. And if he robbed, he was also punished. And if he stole, he was also punished. And if he committed adultery, he was also punished. Yes, for all this wickedness they were punished.

For there was a law that people should be judged according to their crimes. But there was no law against what a person believed. Therefore, a person was only punished for the crimes he had committed, and so all people were on equal ground.
And this Anti-Christ, whose name was Korihor (and the law could not touch him), began to preach to the people that there should be no Christ. And this is the kind of thing he preached, saying:
You who are weighed down by a foolish and empty hope, why do you burden yourselves with such foolish things? Why do you look for a Christ? No one can know anything about what is going to happen.
Look, these things you call prophecies, which you say were handed down by holy prophets—they are nothing but foolish traditions of your ancestors.
How do you know they are true? You cannot know about things you do not see. Therefore you cannot know that there will be a Christ.

You look forward and say that you see a forgiveness of your sins. But that is just the effect of a disturbed mind. And this confusion in your minds comes from the traditions of your ancestors, which lead you to believe in things that are not real.

And he said many more things like this to them, telling them that there could be no atonement made for people's sins, but that every person got ahead in this life by their own efforts. Therefore every person prospered according to their own talent, and every person succeeded according to their own strength, and whatever a person did was not a crime.

And so he preached to them, leading away the hearts of many people and causing them to become proud in their wickedness. Yes, he led away many women and men to commit sexual sins, telling them that when a person was dead, that was the end of everything.
Now this man also went over to the land of Jershon to preach these things among the people of Ammon, who had once been Lamanites.
But they were wiser than many of the Nephites, because they seized him, tied him up, and brought him before Ammon, who was a high priest over that people.

And Ammon had him removed from the land. And Korihor went over into the land of Gideon and began to preach to the people there too. But he did not have much success there, because he was seized, tied up, and brought before the high priest and also the chief judge over the land.

And the high priest said to him: Why do you go around twisting the ways of the Lord? Why do you teach this people that there will be no Christ, and interrupt their happiness? Why do you speak against all the prophecies of the holy prophets?

Now the high priest's name was Giddonah. And Korihor said to him: Because I do not teach the foolish traditions of your ancestors, and because I do not teach this people to tie themselves down with the foolish rules and rituals set up by ancient priests to seize power and authority over them, to keep them ignorant so they cannot hold their heads up, but are kept down according to your words.
You say that this people is a free people. But I say they are in slavery. You say that those ancient prophecies are true. But I say that you do not know whether they are true.
You say that this people is a guilty and fallen people because of the sin of a parent. But I say that a child is not guilty because of its parents.
And you also say that Christ will come. But I say that you do not know that there will be a Christ. And you also say that He will be killed for the sins of the world—

And so you lead this people astray after the foolish traditions of your ancestors and according to your own desires. And you keep them down, as if in slavery, so that you can stuff yourselves with the work of their hands, so that they dare not look up with confidence, and so they dare not enjoy their rights and freedoms.

Yes, they dare not use what is their own for fear of offending their priests, who control them according to their own desires and have led them to believe—through their traditions, dreams, whims, visions, and so-called mysteries—that if they do not do what their priests say, they will offend some unknown being who they say is God—a being who has never been seen or known, who never existed and never will.

Now when the high priest and the chief judge saw how hard his heart was—yes, when they saw that he would even speak against God—they would not reply to his words. But they had him tied up and handed him over to the officers, and sent him to the land of Zarahemla to be brought before Alma and the chief judge who was governor over all the land.
And when he was brought before Alma and the chief judge, he continued in the same way as he had in the land of Gideon. Yes, he went on speaking against God.

And he stood up with grand, boastful words before Alma and attacked the priests and teachers, accusing them of leading the people astray with the silly traditions of their ancestors so they could get rich off the people's work.

Now Alma said to him: You know that we do not get rich off the work of this people. I have worked with my own hands to support myself from the very beginning of the reign of the judges until now, despite my many travels throughout the land to declare the word of God to my people.

And despite all the work I have done in the church, I have never received even one senine for my labor, and neither have any of my fellow workers—except when serving as a judge, and then we received only what the law allowed for our time.

And now, if we do not receive anything for our work in the church, what benefit is there for us to work in the church except to declare the truth, so that we may share in the joy of our fellow believers?

Then why do you say that we preach to this people for money, when you yourself know that we receive no money? And now, do you believe that we deceive this people, when it brings them such joy in their hearts?
And Korihor answered him: Yes.
And then Alma said to him: Do you believe that there is a God?
And he answered: No.
Now Alma said to him: Will you deny again that there is a God, and also deny the Christ? For I tell you, I know there is a God, and also that Christ will come.
And now, what evidence do you have that there is no God, or that Christ will not come? I tell you that you have none—only your word.
But I have everything around me as evidence that these things are true, and you also have everything as evidence that they are true. And will you deny them? Do you believe that these things are true?

I know that you do believe, but you are controlled by a lying spirit, and you have pushed away the Spirit of God so that it has no place in you. But the devil has power over you, and he leads you around, using tricks to destroy the children of God.
And now Korihor said to Alma: If you will show me a sign so that I can be convinced that there is a God—yes, show me that He has power—and then I will be convinced that your words are true.

But Alma said to him: You have had enough signs already. Will you test your God? Will you say, 'Show me a sign,' when you have the testimony of all these people around you, and also all the holy prophets? The scriptures are right in front of you. Yes, and everything shows that there is a God—yes, even the earth and everything on it, yes, and the way it moves, and also all the planets that move in their regular patterns are proof that there is a Supreme Creator.

And yet you go around leading away the hearts of this people, telling them there is no God? And will you still deny in the face of all this evidence? And he said: Yes, I will deny it unless you show me a sign.
And then Alma said to him: I am deeply troubled because of the hardness of your heart—that you keep resisting the spirit of truth so that your soul may be destroyed.

But it is better that your soul should be lost than that you should be the means of destroying many souls through your lying and flattering words. Therefore, if you deny again, God will strike you so that you become unable to speak, so that you will never open your mouth again and never deceive this people anymore.

Now Korihor said to him: I do not deny that a God exists, but I do not believe that there is a God. And I also say that you do not know that there is a God. And unless you show me a sign, I will not believe.

Now Alma said to him: I will give you this as a sign: you will be struck unable to speak, according to my words. And I say that in the name of God, you will be struck unable to speak, so that you will no longer be able to say a word.
Now when Alma had said these words, Korihor was struck unable to speak, so that he could not say a word, just as Alma had said.

And when the chief judge saw this, he reached out his hand and wrote to Korihor, saying: Are you now convinced of the power of God? Who did you want Alma to show his sign through? Would you have had him afflict other people to show you a sign? He has shown you a sign. And now will you argue any more?

And Korihor reached out his hand and wrote, saying: I know that I am unable to speak, because I cannot say a word. And I know that nothing except the power of God could have brought this upon me. Yes, and I always knew that there was a God.

But the devil deceived me, because he appeared to me in the form of an angel and said to me: Go and reclaim this people, because they have all gone astray after an unknown God. And he said to me: There is no God. Yes, and he taught me what I should say. And I taught his words, and I taught them because they were pleasing to the natural, worldly mind. And I taught them until I had great success, so much that I truly believed they were true. And for this reason I resisted the truth, until I have brought this great curse upon myself.
Now when he had said this, he begged Alma to pray to God to have the curse removed from him.
But Alma said to him: If this curse were taken from you, you would lead away the hearts of this people again. Therefore, it will be done to you as the Lord decides.
And the curse was not taken from Korihor. But he was cast out and went from house to house begging for food.

Now the news of what had happened to Korihor spread immediately throughout all the land. Yes, the chief judge sent out a proclamation to all the people in the land, declaring to those who had believed Korihor's words that they must repent quickly, or the same judgments would come upon them.

And they were all convinced of Korihor's wickedness, so they all turned back to the Lord. And this put an end to the wickedness that followed the pattern of Korihor. And Korihor went from house to house, begging for food to survive.

And as he traveled among the people—yes, among a people who had separated themselves from the Nephites and called themselves Zoramites, being led by a man named Zoram—as he went among them, he was trampled and crushed until he was dead.
And so we see the end of someone who twists the ways of the Lord. And so we see that the devil will not support his followers in the last day but quickly drags them down to hell.

31.text: Now after Korihor's end, Alma received news that the Zoramites were twisting the ways of the Lord and that Zoram, their leader, was leading the hearts of the people to bow down to worthless idols. Alma's heart was troubled again because of the wickedness of the people.
For it caused Alma great sorrow to know about wickedness among his people. Therefore his heart was deeply sorrowful because the Zoramites had separated from the Nephites.

Now the Zoramites had gathered together in a land they called Antionum, which was east of the land of Zarahemla, lying nearly along the seashore, south of the land of Jershon, which also bordered the wilderness to the south—a wilderness that was full of Lamanites.
Now the Nephites were very afraid that the Zoramites would form an alliance with the Lamanites, and that this would lead to a great loss for the Nephites.

And now, since preaching the word had a strong tendency to lead people to do what was right—yes, it had a more powerful effect on people's minds than the sword or anything else that had been tried—Alma thought it was best to try the power of the word of God.

So he took Ammon, Aaron, and Omner with him. He left Himni behind to take care of the church in Zarahemla, but the other three he took along, as well as Amulek and Zeezrom, who were at Melek. He also took two of his sons.

He did not take his eldest son with him—his name was Helaman—but the sons he took were named Shiblon and Corianton. And these are the names of those who went with him among the Zoramites to preach the word to them.
Now the Zoramites had broken away from the Nephites, so they had already heard the word of God preached to them before.
But they had fallen into serious errors, because they refused to keep the commandments of God and His rules according to the law of Moses.
They also would not follow the practices of the church, such as continuing in daily prayer and asking God for help so that they would not fall into temptation.
In short, they twisted the ways of the Lord in many ways. For this reason, Alma and his companions went into the land to preach the word to them.

Now when they arrived in the land, they were astonished to find that the Zoramites had built synagogues and gathered together on one day of the week, which they called the day of the Lord. And they worshipped in a way that Alma and his companions had never seen before.
For they had built a platform in the center of their synagogue—a place for standing that was raised high above everyone's head—and only one person at a time could stand on top of it.
So whoever wanted to worship had to go up and stand on top of the platform, stretch out their hands toward heaven, and cry out in a loud voice, saying:
Holy, holy God—we believe that You are God, and we believe that You are holy, and that You were a spirit, and that You are a spirit, and that You will be a spirit forever.

Holy God, we believe that You have separated us from our fellow people. And we do not believe in the traditions of our fellow people, which were handed down to them through the foolishness of their ancestors. But we believe that You have chosen us to be Your holy children. And You have also made it known to us that there will be no Christ.

But You are the same yesterday, today, and forever. And You have chosen us so that we will be saved, while everyone around us is chosen to be thrown down to hell by Your anger. For this holiness, O God, we thank You. And we also thank You that You have chosen us so that we will not be led away by the foolish traditions of our fellow people, which tie them down to a belief in Christ—a belief that causes their hearts to wander far from You, our God.
And again we thank You, O God, that we are a chosen and holy people. Amen.
Now after Alma, his companions, and his sons had heard these prayers, they were shocked beyond belief.
For every single person went up and said these exact same prayers.
Now they called the platform Rameumptom, which means "the holy stand."

From this stand, every person offered the exact same prayer to God, thanking their God that He had chosen them, that He did not lead them away by the traditions of their fellow people, and that their hearts were not drawn to believe in things to come that they knew nothing about.

Now after all the people had offered thanks in this way, they returned to their homes and never spoke of their God again until they gathered together again at the holy stand to offer thanks in this same way.
When Alma saw this, his heart was deeply troubled, because he saw that they were a wicked and corrupt people. Yes, he saw that their hearts were set on gold, silver, and all kinds of expensive goods.
Yes, and he also saw that their hearts were full of great pride and boasting.
And he lifted his voice to heaven and cried out, saying: O how long, O Lord, will You allow Your servants to live here on earth and witness such terrible wickedness among the children of men?
Look, O God, they cry out to You, yet their hearts are consumed with pride. Look, O God, they cry out to You with their mouths while they are puffed up with the worthless things of the world.

Look, O my God, at their expensive clothing, their jewelry, their bracelets, their gold ornaments, and all their precious things that they decorate themselves with. Their hearts are set on these things, and yet they cry out to You and say: We thank You, O God, for we are a chosen people, while everyone else will be destroyed.
Yes, and they say that You have made it known to them that there will be no Christ.

O Lord God, how long will You allow such wickedness and unfaithfulness among this people? O Lord, will You give me strength to bear my weaknesses? For I am weak, and such wickedness among this people causes me deep pain.

O Lord, my heart is extremely sorrowful. Will You comfort my soul in Christ? O Lord, will You grant me strength so I can endure with patience the hardships that will come upon me because of the wickedness of this people?

O Lord, will You comfort my soul and give me success, and also my fellow workers who are with me—yes, Ammon, Aaron, Omner, and also Amulek and Zeezrom, and also my two sons—yes, will You comfort all of them, O Lord? Will You comfort their souls in Christ?
Will You grant them strength to bear the hardships that will come upon them because of the sins of this people?
O Lord, will You grant us success in bringing them back to You through Christ?
Their souls are precious, O Lord, and many of them are our own people. So give us, O Lord, the power and wisdom to bring them, our people, back to You.
Now after Alma had said these words, he placed his hands on all those who were with him. And as he placed his hands on them, they were filled with the Holy Spirit.
And after that, they separated from one another, not worrying about what they would eat, drink, or wear.

And the Lord provided for them so that they would not go hungry or thirsty. Yes, and He also gave them strength so that they would not suffer any hardships except those that were swallowed up in the joy of Christ. Now this was in answer to Alma's prayer, and it happened because he prayed in faith.

32.text: And they went out and began to preach the word of God to the people, entering their synagogues and their homes. Yes, they even preached the word in the streets.
And after much hard work among them, they began to have success among the poor people, because these poor were cast out of the synagogues because of their rough clothing.

So they were not allowed to enter the synagogues to worship God, because they were looked down upon as worthless. They were poor, yes, and they were treated by their own people like trash. So they were poor when it came to worldly things, and they were also humble in heart.

Now while Alma was teaching and speaking to the people on the hill Onidah, a large crowd came to him—the very people we have been talking about, those who were humble in heart because of their worldly poverty.

And they came to Alma, and the leader among them said to him: What should our people do? We are looked down on by everyone because of our poverty, especially by our priests. They have thrown us out of the synagogues that we worked so hard to build with our own hands. They have cast us out because of our extreme poverty. And we have no place to worship our God. So what should we do?

And when Alma heard this, he immediately turned to face the man, and he looked at them with great joy, because he saw that their hardships had truly made them humble and that they were ready to hear the word.
So he said nothing more to the other crowd. Instead, he reached out his hand and called to those he could see were truly sorry for their sins, and said to them:
I can see that you are humble in heart, and if so, you are blessed.
Your brother has asked: What should we do? We are cast out of our synagogues and cannot worship our God.
I say to you: Do you think you can only worship God in your synagogues?
And also, do you think you should only worship God once a week?

I say to you, it is actually good that you were cast out of your synagogues, so that you can be humble and learn wisdom. For it is necessary that you learn wisdom. It is because you are cast out and looked down on by your people because of your extreme poverty that you are brought to humility of heart. You are necessarily brought to being humble.

And now, because you are forced to be humble, you are blessed. For a person, if forced to be humble, sometimes seeks repentance. And whoever repents will certainly find mercy. And whoever finds mercy and endures to the end will be saved.
And now, as I said, because you were forced to be humble you were blessed. But don't you think that those who truly humble themselves because of the word are even more blessed?
Yes, the person who truly humbles himself, repents of his sins, and endures to the end will be blessed—yes, much more blessed than those who are forced to be humble because of their extreme poverty.

Therefore, blessed are those who humble themselves without being forced to be humble. Or in other words, blessed is the person who believes in the word of God and is baptized without stubbornness of heart—yes, without needing to be shown or forced to know the word before they will believe.
Yes, there are many who say: If you show us a sign from heaven, then we will know for sure, and then we will believe.
Now I ask, is this faith? I tell you: No. Because if a person knows something, he has no reason to believe—he already knows it.
And how much worse off is the person who knows the will of God and does not do it, compared to the person who only believes or only has reason to believe, and falls into sin?
Now you must judge this for yourselves. I tell you that it is the same on both sides—everyone will be rewarded according to what they do.
And now, as I was saying about faith: faith is not to have a perfect knowledge of things. Therefore, if you have faith, you hope for things which you cannot see but which are true.
And now I say to you—and I want you to remember this—God is merciful to all who believe in His name. Therefore His first desire is that you believe, yes, even in His word.
And now, He shares His word through angels to men—yes, not only men but women also. And that is not all; little children often receive words given to them that confound the wise and the educated.
And now, my dear friends, since you have wanted to know what you should do because you are afflicted and cast out—I don't want you to think I mean to judge you only by what is true—
For I do not mean that all of you have been forced to humble yourselves. I truly believe that there are some among you who would humble themselves no matter what circumstances they were in.

Now, as I said about faith—that it was not a perfect knowledge—even so it is with my words. You cannot know with complete certainty at first that they are true, any more than faith is a perfect knowledge.

But if you will wake up and stir yourselves to try an experiment with my words, and exercise even a tiny bit of faith—yes, even if you can do no more than desire to believe—let that desire work in you until you believe enough to make room for a portion of my words.

Now, we will compare the word to a seed. If you make room for a seed to be planted in your heart, and if it is a true seed, a good seed, and you do not cast it out by your unbelief so as to resist the Spirit of the Lord, it will begin to grow within you. And when you feel it growing, you will begin to say to yourself: This must be a good seed, or the word must be good, because it is beginning to enlarge my soul. Yes, it is beginning to enlighten my understanding. Yes, it is beginning to feel wonderful to me.
Now, would not this increase your faith? I tell you: Yes. But it has not yet grown into a perfect knowledge.

But as the seed swells, sprouts, and begins to grow, then you have to admit that the seed is good, because it is swelling, sprouting, and beginning to grow. And now, will this not strengthen your faith? Yes, it will strengthen your faith, because you will say: I know that this is a good seed, because it is sprouting and beginning to grow.
And now, can you be sure that this is a good seed? I tell you: Yes, because every seed produces after its own kind.
Therefore, if a seed grows it is good, but if it does not grow, it is not good and is thrown away.
And now, because you have tried the experiment and planted the seed, and it swells, sprouts, and begins to grow, you must know that the seed is good.

And now, is your knowledge perfect? Yes, your knowledge is perfect in that one thing, and your faith becomes inactive, because you now know. For you know that the word has enlarged your souls, and you also know that it has sprouted up and that your understanding is beginning to be enlightened and your mind is beginning to expand.

Oh then, is this not real? I tell you: Yes, because it is light. And whatever is light is good, because it can be clearly seen. Therefore you must know that it is good. And now, after you have tasted this light, is your knowledge perfect?
I tell you: No. And you must not set aside your faith either, because you have only used your faith to plant the seed so you could test whether the seed was good.

And as the tree begins to grow, you will say: Let us take great care of it so that it may take root and grow up and produce fruit for us. And if you nourish it with great care, it will take root and grow up and produce fruit.

But if you neglect the tree and do not take care of it, it will not take root. And when the heat of the sun comes and scorches it, because it has no root, it dries up, and you pull it up and throw it away.

Now, this is not because the seed was bad, nor is it because the fruit would not have been worth having. But it is because your soil is barren, and you would not nourish the tree. So you cannot have the fruit.
And so, if you will not nourish the word, looking forward with an eye of faith to the fruit it will produce, you can never pick the fruit of the tree of life.

But if you will nourish the word—yes, nourish the tree as it begins to grow, by your faith with great effort and patience, looking forward to its fruit—it will take root. And it will become a tree springing up to everlasting life.

And because of your effort, your faith, and your patience in nourishing the word so that it may take root in you, in time you will pick the fruit, which is the most precious fruit, sweeter than anything sweet, whiter than anything white, yes, and purer than anything pure. And you will feast on this fruit until you are full, and you will never hunger or thirst again.
Then, my friends, you will receive the rewards of your faith, your effort, your patience, and your long-suffering, waiting for the tree to produce its fruit for you.

33.text: Now after Alma had spoken these words, the people asked him whether they should believe in one God so they could obtain the fruit he had spoken of, or how they should plant the seed—the word he had spoken of—which he said must be planted in their hearts, or in what way they should begin to exercise their faith.

And Alma said to them: You have said that you cannot worship your God because you are cast out of your synagogues. But I tell you, if you think you cannot worship God, you are greatly mistaken, and you should search the scriptures. If you think the scriptures have taught you this, you do not understand them.
Do you remember reading what Zenos, the ancient prophet, said about prayer and worship?
For he said: You are merciful, O God, because You heard my prayer even when I was in the wilderness. Yes, You were merciful when I prayed about my enemies, and You turned them to me.
Yes, O God, and You were merciful to me when I cried out to You in my field. When I cried out to You in my prayer, You heard me.
And again, O God, when I turned to go to my house, You heard me in my prayer.
And when I went into my private room, O Lord, and prayed to You, You heard me.
Yes, You are merciful to Your children when they cry out to You, wanting to be heard by You and not by men, and You will hear them.
Yes, O God, You have been merciful to me and heard my cries in the midst of Your congregations.
Yes, and You have also heard me when I was cast out and looked down on by my enemies. Yes, You heard my cries and were angry with my enemies, and You punished them with swift destruction.

And You heard me because of my suffering and my sincerity. And it is because of Your Son that You have been so merciful to me. Therefore I will cry out to You in all my troubles, because in You is my joy. For You have turned Your judgments away from me because of Your Son.
And now Alma said to them: Do you believe those scriptures that were written by people of long ago?
If you do, you must believe what Zenos said, for he said: You have turned away Your judgments because of Your Son.
Now my friends, I would ask: Have you read the scriptures? If you have, how can you not believe in the Son of God?
For it is not only Zenos who spoke of these things—Zenock also spoke of them.
For he said: You are angry, O Lord, with this people, because they will not understand the mercies You have given them because of Your Son.
And now, my friends, you can see that a second ancient prophet testified of the Son of God. And because the people would not understand his words, they stoned him to death.
But this is not all. These are not the only ones who have spoken about the Son of God.
Moses also spoke of Him. Yes, and a symbol was raised up in the wilderness, so that whoever would look at it could live. And many did look and lived.

But few understood the meaning of those things, and this was because of the hardness of their hearts. But there were many who were so hardened that they would not look, and so they died. The reason they would not look was because they did not believe it would heal them.
O my friends, if you could be healed simply by looking, would you not look quickly? Or would you rather harden your hearts in unbelief and be too lazy to look, choosing to die instead?

If so, sorrow will come upon you. But if not, then look up and begin to believe in the Son of God—that He will come to save His people, that He will suffer and die to pay for their sins, and that He will rise again from the dead, bringing about the resurrection, so that everyone will stand before Him to be judged on the last judgment day according to what they have done.

And now, my friends, I ask that you plant this word in your hearts, and as it begins to grow, nourish it by your faith. And it will become a tree, springing up in you to everlasting life. And then may God grant that your burdens may be light through the joy of His Son. And you can do all of this if you will. Amen.

34.text: And now after Alma had spoken these words to them, he sat down on the ground, and Amulek stood up and began to teach them, saying:

My friends, I think it is impossible that you could be unaware of the things that have been said about the coming of Christ, who we teach is the Son of God. Yes, I know that these things were taught to you many times before you separated from us.

And since you have asked my dear brother to tell you what you should do because of your troubles, he has spoken to you to prepare your minds. Yes, and he has encouraged you to have faith and to be patient—
Yes, even to have enough faith to plant the word in your hearts so that you can test its goodness.
And we have seen that the big question on your minds is whether the word centers on the Son of God, or whether there will be no Christ.
And you have also seen that my brother has proved to you, in many ways, that the word points to Christ for salvation.
My brother quoted the words of Zenos—that redemption comes through the Son of God—and also the words of Zenock. And he also pointed to Moses to prove that these things are true.

And now I will testify to you myself that these things are true. I tell you that I do know that Christ will come among the children of men to take upon Himself the sins of His people, and that He will pay for the sins of the world, for the Lord God has spoken it.

For it is necessary that an atonement be made. For according to the great plan of the Eternal God, an atonement must be made, or else all mankind will certainly be lost. Yes, all are hardened, all have fallen and are lost, and will be destroyed unless it is through the atonement that must be made.

For it is necessary that there be a great and last sacrifice—yes, not a sacrifice of a person, or an animal, or any kind of bird. For it will not be a human sacrifice, but it must be an infinite and eternal sacrifice.
Now there is no person who can sacrifice his own blood to pay for the sins of another. Now, if a person murders someone, would our law, which is just, take the life of his brother? I tell you: No.
But the law requires the life of the one who murdered. Therefore, nothing less than an infinite atonement will be enough to pay for the sins of the world.

Therefore, it is necessary that there be a great and last sacrifice, and then there will be—or rather, it is necessary that there be—an end to the shedding of blood. Then the law of Moses will be fulfilled. Yes, it will all be fulfilled, every small detail, and nothing will be left undone.
And this is the entire meaning of the law—every part of it points to that great and last sacrifice. And that great and last sacrifice will be the Son of God—yes, infinite and eternal.

And so He will bring salvation to all those who believe in His name. This is the purpose of this last sacrifice—to bring about the tender mercy that overpowers justice and provides a way for people to have faith that leads to repentance.

And so mercy can satisfy the demands of justice and wraps people in the arms of safety. But the person who does not exercise faith that leads to repentance is exposed to the full demands of justice. Therefore, only the person who has faith that leads to repentance benefits from the great and eternal plan of redemption.
Therefore may God grant, my friends, that you may begin to exercise your faith that leads to repentance, that you begin to call upon His holy name, asking Him to have mercy on you.
Yes, cry out to Him for mercy, for He is mighty to save.
Yes, humble yourselves and continue in prayer to Him.
Cry out to Him when you are in your fields, yes, over all your flocks.
Cry out to Him in your homes, yes, over all your household, both morning, midday, and evening.
Yes, cry out to Him against the power of your enemies.
Yes, cry out to Him against the devil, who is an enemy to all righteousness.
Cry out to Him over the crops of your fields, that you may prosper.
Cry out over the flocks of your fields, that they may increase.
But this is not all. You must pour out your souls in your private rooms, your secret places, and in the wilderness.
Yes, and when you are not crying out to the Lord, let your hearts be full, constantly drawn out in prayer to Him for your own well-being and also for the well-being of those around you.

And now, my dear friends, I say to you: Do not think that this is all. For after you have done all these things, if you turn away the needy and the poorly clothed, and do not visit the sick and afflicted, and do not share what you have with those in need—I say to you, if you do not do any of these things, then your prayer is worthless and does you no good, and you are like hypocrites who deny the faith.
Therefore, if you do not remember to be charitable, you are like waste material that metalworkers throw out as worthless, to be trampled underfoot by men.
And now, my friends, since you have received so many witnesses, and since the holy scriptures testify of these things, I ask that you come forward and bring forth the fruits of repentance.

Yes, I ask that you come forward and harden your hearts no longer. For now is the time and the day of your salvation. And therefore, if you will repent and not harden your hearts, the great plan of redemption will immediately be brought about for you.
For this life is the time for people to prepare to meet God. Yes, this day of life is the day for people to do their work.

And now, as I said before, since you have had so many witnesses, I beg you not to put off the day of your repentance until the end. For after this day of life, which is given to us to prepare for eternity, if we do not make good use of our time while in this life, then the night of darkness comes when no work can be done.

You cannot say, when you are brought to that terrible moment, that you will repent or that you will return to God. No, you cannot say this. For the same spirit that has control of your body at the time you leave this life will have the power to control you in the eternal world.

For if you have put off the day of your repentance even until death, you have become subject to the spirit of the devil, and he claims you as his own. Therefore, the Spirit of the Lord has withdrawn from you and has no place in you, and the devil has all power over you. And this is the final state of the wicked.

And I know this because the Lord has said that He does not dwell in unholy temples, but dwells in the hearts of the righteous. Yes, and He has also said that the righteous will sit down in His kingdom, never to leave it again, and their garments will be made white through the blood of the Lamb.
And now, my dear friends, I ask that you remember these things and that you work out your salvation with reverence before God, and that you no longer deny the coming of Christ.

Do not fight against the Holy Ghost anymore, but receive it and take upon you the name of Christ. Humble yourselves even to the dust and worship God, wherever you may be, in spirit and in truth. And live in thankfulness every day for the many mercies and blessings He gives you.

Yes, and I also encourage you, my friends, to be always watchful in prayer, so that you are not led away by the temptations of the devil and he does not overpower you and you do not become his subjects in the last day. For he gives you nothing good.

And now, my dear friends, I encourage you to have patience and to endure all kinds of hardships. Do not speak badly against those who cast you out because of your extreme poverty, or you will become sinners like them.
But have patience and bear those hardships with a firm hope that one day you will rest from all your troubles.

35.text: After Amulek finished speaking, they left the crowd and went to the land of Jershon.
And the rest of the brethren, after they had preached the word to the Zoramites, also came to the land of Jershon.

After the more influential Zoramites talked together about the words that had been preached to them, they were angry because of the message, since it threatened their power and wealth. So they refused to listen.
They sent messengers and gathered all the people throughout the land, and consulted with them about the words that had been spoken.
Now their rulers, priests, and teachers did not let the people know what they were planning. Instead, they secretly found out what all the people were thinking.

After they learned the feelings of all the people, those who supported the words spoken by Alma and his brethren were thrown out of the land. There were many of them, and they also came to the land of Jershon.
And Alma and his brethren served and cared for them.

Now the Zoramites were angry with the people of Ammon who lived in Jershon, and the chief ruler of the Zoramites, who was a very wicked man, sent a message to the people of Ammon demanding that they throw out of their land everyone who had come from the Zoramites.

He made many threats against them. But the people of Ammon were not afraid of his words, so they did not cast them out. Instead, they welcomed all the poor Zoramites who came to them. They fed them, clothed them, gave them land as an inheritance, and took care of their needs.
This made the Zoramites even more angry at the people of Ammon, and they began to join forces with the Lamanites and stir them up to anger as well.
And so the Zoramites and the Lamanites began preparing for war against the people of Ammon and the Nephites.
And so ended the seventeenth year of the reign of the judges over the people of Nephi.

The people of Ammon left the land of Jershon and moved to the land of Melek, making room in Jershon for the Nephite armies so they could fight the Lamanite and Zoramite armies. And so a war began between the Lamanites and the Nephites in the eighteenth year of the reign of the judges, and an account of their wars will be given later.

Alma, Ammon, their brethren, and Alma's two sons returned to the land of Zarahemla. They had been instruments in God's hands in bringing many Zoramites to repentance. All who repented were driven out of their land, but they received land as an inheritance in Jershon, and they took up arms to defend themselves, their wives, their children, and their lands.

Now Alma was deeply grieved because of the wickedness of his people—the wars, the bloodshed, and the conflicts among them. He had gone out, or had been sent, to preach the word among all the people in every city. And seeing that the hearts of the people were growing hard and that they were becoming offended by the demands of the word, his heart was filled with sorrow.

Therefore, he gathered his sons together so he could give each one his own personal counsel about the things of righteousness. And we have a record of the instructions he gave them, written in his own words.

36.text: My son, listen to my words, for I promise you that as long as you keep the commandments of God, you will prosper in the land.

I want you to do as I have done, by remembering how our fathers were held captive. They were in bondage, and no one could free them except the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob—and he truly did deliver them in their afflictions.

And now, my son Helaman, you are young, and so I beg you to listen to my words and learn from me. For I know that whoever puts their trust in God will be supported through their trials, troubles, and afflictions, and will be lifted up at the last day.
I do not want you to think that I know these things on my own—not through worldly understanding but through spiritual knowledge, not from my own reasoning but from God.
If I had not been born of God, I would not have known these things. But God, through his holy angel, made these things known to me—not because of any worthiness of my own.
For I went around with the sons of Mosiah, trying to destroy the church of God. But God sent his holy angel to stop us along the way.
And he spoke to us with a voice like thunder, and the whole earth trembled beneath our feet. We all fell to the ground, because the fear of the Lord came upon us.
But the voice said to me: Stand up. And I stood up and saw the angel.
And he said to me: Even if you want to destroy yourself, stop trying to destroy the church of God.
And I fell to the earth, and for three days and three nights I could not open my mouth or move my body.

The angel said more things that my brethren heard, but I did not hear them. For when I heard the words—Even if you want to destroy yourself, stop trying to destroy the church of God—I was struck with such overwhelming fear and shock that I might be destroyed, that I fell to the earth and heard nothing more.
But I was tortured with eternal torment, for my soul was torn apart to the greatest degree and weighed down with all my sins.
Yes, I remembered all my sins and wrongdoings, and I was tormented with the pains of hell. I saw that I had rebelled against my God and had not kept his holy commandments.
Yes, and I had led many of his children to spiritual destruction. My sins had been so great that the very thought of coming into the presence of my God filled my soul with unspeakable horror.
Oh, I thought, if only I could be cast away and cease to exist, both soul and body, so that I would not have to stand in the presence of my God to be judged for what I had done.
And for three days and three nights I was tortured, even with the pains of a condemned soul.

And while I was being tortured, while I was weighed down by the memory of my many sins, I also remembered hearing my father prophesy to the people about the coming of one Jesus Christ, a Son of God, who would atone for the sins of the world.

As my mind grabbed hold of this thought, I cried out within my heart: O Jesus, thou Son of God, have mercy on me, who am in the gall of bitterness, and am encircled about by the everlasting chains of death.
And when I thought this, I could not remember my pains anymore. I was no longer tormented by the memory of my sins.
And oh, what joy, and what marvelous light I saw! My soul was filled with joy as intense as my pain had been!
I tell you, my son, that nothing could have been as sharp and bitter as my pains. And again I tell you, my son, that on the other hand, nothing could have been as sharp and sweet as my joy.
I thought I saw, just as our father Lehi saw, God sitting upon his throne, surrounded by countless hosts of angels, singing and praising their God. And my soul longed to be there.
But my body received its strength again, and I stood on my feet and told the people that I had been born of God.

And from that time until now, I have worked without stopping to bring souls to repentance—so they could taste the same incredible joy I tasted, so they too could be born of God and be filled with the Holy Ghost.
And now, my son, the Lord gives me tremendous joy in the results of my labors.

Because of the word he has given me, many have been born of God and have tasted what I have tasted and have seen what I have seen. Therefore they know the things I have spoken of, just as I know them, and the knowledge I have comes from God.

And I have been sustained through trials and troubles of every kind—yes, through all kinds of afflictions. God has delivered me from prison, from chains, and from death. I put my trust in him, and he will continue to deliver me.

And I know that he will raise me up at the last day to live with him in glory. I will praise him forever, for he brought our fathers out of Egypt and swallowed up the Egyptians in the Red Sea. He led them by his power into the promised land, and he has delivered them out of bondage and captivity again and again.

He also brought our fathers out of the land of Jerusalem, and by his everlasting power he has delivered them out of bondage and captivity from time to time, even down to the present day. I have always kept the memory of their captivity in my mind, and you should remember it too, just as I have.

But this is not all, my son. You should know, as I know, that as long as you keep the commandments of God you will prosper in the land. And you should also know that if you do not keep the commandments of God, you will be cut off from his presence. This is according to his word.

37.text: And now, my son Helaman, I command you to take the records that have been entrusted to me.
I also command you to keep a record of this people, as I have done, on the plates of Nephi, and keep all these things sacred, just as I have kept them. For they are kept for a wise purpose.
These plates of brass, which contain these engravings—which have the records of the holy scriptures and the genealogy of our forefathers from the very beginning—

our fathers prophesied that they should be kept and handed down from one generation to another, preserved by the hand of the Lord until they go forth to every nation, people, language, and culture, so that all may know the sacred truths contained in them.
And if they are kept, they will retain their brightness. Yes, they will retain their brightness, as will all the plates that contain holy scripture.
Now you may think this seems foolish to me, but I tell you that by small and simple things, great things are brought to pass. And small means often confound the wise.
The Lord God works through simple means to accomplish his great and eternal purposes. By very small means, the Lord confounds the wise and brings about the salvation of many souls.

Up to now, it has been wise in God's eyes for these things to be preserved, for they have strengthened the memory of this people, convinced many of the error of their ways, and brought them to the knowledge of their God and the salvation of their souls.

I tell you, if it were not for these records on these plates, Ammon and his brethren could not have convinced so many thousands of Lamanites that their fathers' traditions were wrong. These records and their words brought them to repentance—brought them to know the Lord their God and to rejoice in Jesus Christ their Redeemer.

And who knows whether these records will also bring many thousands of them—and also many thousands of our stubborn Nephite brethren, who are now hardening their hearts in sin—to the knowledge of their Redeemer?
These purposes are not yet fully made known to me, so I will say no more about them.

Let it be enough to say that they are preserved for a wise purpose, a purpose known to God. For he counsels in wisdom over all his works, and his paths are straight, and his course is one eternal round.

O remember, remember, my son Helaman, how strict the commandments of God are. And he said: If you will keep my commandments you will prosper in the land—but if you do not keep his commandments you will be cut off from his presence.

And now remember, my son, that God has entrusted you with these sacred things, which he has kept sacred and which he will continue to keep and preserve for a wise purpose, so he may show his power to future generations.

And now I tell you by the spirit of prophecy: if you break the commandments of God, these sacred things will be taken from you by the power of God, and you will be handed over to Satan, who will toss you about like chaff in the wind.

But if you keep the commandments of God and handle these sacred things according to what the Lord commands you—for you must seek the Lord's guidance in everything you do with them—then no power on earth or in hell can take them from you, for God is powerful enough to fulfill all his words.
For he will fulfill all the promises he makes to you, for he has fulfilled the promises he made to our fathers.
He promised them that he would preserve these things for a wise purpose, so he could show his power to future generations.

And he has already fulfilled one of those purposes—the restoration of many thousands of Lamanites to the knowledge of the truth. He has shown his power among them, and he will continue to show his power among them in future generations. Therefore, these records will be preserved.
Therefore I command you, my son Helaman, to be careful to do everything I have told you, and to be careful to keep the commandments of God as they are written.

And now I will tell you about those twenty-four plates. Keep them, so that the hidden plans and works of darkness—the secret practices of those people who were destroyed—may be revealed to this people. All their murders, robberies, plundering, wickedness, and abominations may be made known to this people. Also preserve the interpreters.

For the Lord saw that his people began to work in darkness, carrying out secret murders and abominations. Therefore the Lord said that if they did not repent, they would be destroyed from the face of the earth.

And the Lord said: I will prepare for my servant Gazelem a stone that will shine in darkness to give light, so that I may reveal to my people who serve me the secret works of their brethren—their works of darkness, wickedness, and abominations.
And now, my son, these interpreters were prepared so that the word of God might be fulfilled, when he said:

I will bring all their secret works and abominations out of darkness into light. And unless they repent, I will destroy them from the face of the earth. I will reveal all their secrets and abominations to every nation that will afterward possess the land.

And now, my son, we can see that they did not repent. Therefore they were destroyed, and so far the word of God has been fulfilled—their secret abominations have been brought out of darkness and made known to us.

And now, my son, I command you to keep all their oaths, covenants, and agreements related to their secret abominations—all their signs and secret practices—away from this people, so they do not learn them and perhaps fall into darkness and be destroyed as well.

For there is a curse upon this entire land: destruction will come upon all those who work in darkness, according to the power of God, when they are fully ripened in wickedness. Therefore I want this people to avoid being destroyed.

So you must keep the secret plans, oaths, and covenants hidden from this people. Only their wickedness, murders, and abominations should be made known to them. Teach them to hate such wickedness, abominations, and murders. Also teach them that those people were destroyed because of their wickedness, abominations, and murders.

For they murdered all the prophets of the Lord who came to warn them about their sins. And the blood of those they murdered cried out to the Lord their God for justice against their murderers. And so the judgments of God came upon those workers of darkness and secret combinations.
Yes, and the land will be cursed forever for those who practice darkness and secret combinations, even to their destruction, unless they repent before they are fully ripened in wickedness.
And now, my son, remember the words I have spoken to you. Do not entrust those secret plans to this people, but teach them an everlasting hatred of sin and wickedness.

Preach to them repentance and faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. Teach them to humble themselves and to be meek and lowly in heart. Teach them to resist every temptation of the devil through their faith in the Lord Jesus Christ.
Teach them to never grow tired of doing good, but to be meek and lowly in heart, for those who are will find rest for their souls.
O remember, my son, and learn wisdom while you are young. Yes, learn in your youth to keep the commandments of God.

Cry out to God for all your support. Let everything you do be for the Lord, and wherever you go, let it be in the Lord. Let all your thoughts be directed to the Lord, and let your heart be set upon the Lord forever.

Seek the Lord's guidance in everything you do, and he will direct you for good. When you lie down at night, lie down trusting in the Lord, so he may watch over you as you sleep. And when you rise in the morning, let your heart be full of thanks to God. If you do these things, you will be lifted up at the last day.
And now, my son, I have something to say about the thing our fathers called a ball, or director—our fathers called it Liahona, which means a compass—and the Lord prepared it.
No person could have made anything with such remarkable workmanship. It was prepared to show our fathers which direction to travel in the wilderness.

It worked for them according to their faith in God. If they had faith to believe that God could cause those spindles to point the way they should go, then it happened. And so they had this miracle, and also many other miracles performed by the power of God, day by day.

Yet because those miracles were worked through small and simple means, it showed them marvelous things. But when they became lazy and forgot to exercise their faith and diligence, those marvelous works stopped, and they made no progress on their journey.
Because of this, they wandered in the wilderness and did not travel a direct path, and they suffered hunger and thirst because of their disobedience.

And now, my son, I want you to understand that these things are symbolic. Just as our fathers were careless about following this compass—which was a physical thing—they did not prosper. It is the same way with spiritual things.

For it is just as easy to follow the word of Christ, which will point you on a straight course to eternal happiness, as it was for our fathers to follow this compass, which pointed them on a straight course to the promised land.

And now I ask, is there not a lesson in this? For just as surely as this director brought our fathers to the promised land by following its course, the words of Christ, if we follow their course, will carry us beyond this valley of sorrow into a far better promised land.

O my son, do not let us be lazy because the way seems easy. For so it was with our fathers—it was prepared for them so that if they would look, they could live. It is the same with us. The way is prepared, and if we will look, we may live forever.
And now, my son, make sure you take care of these sacred things. Look to God and live. Go to this people and declare the word, and be serious about it. My son, farewell.

38.text: My son, listen to my words, for I say to you, just as I said to Helaman, that as long as you keep the commandments of God you will prosper in the land, and if you do not keep the commandments of God you will be cut off from his presence.

And now, my son, I trust that I will have great joy because of you, because of your steadiness and your faithfulness to God. Since you began looking to the Lord your God in your youth, I hope you will continue keeping his commandments, for blessed is the one who endures to the end.
I tell you, my son, that I have already had great joy because of you—because of your faithfulness, your hard work, your patience, and your endurance among the Zoramites.

I know that you were thrown in prison. I also know that you were stoned for preaching the word. And you endured all these things with patience because the Lord was with you. And now you know that the Lord delivered you.

And now my son, Shiblon, I want you to remember that the more you put your trust in God, the more you will be delivered from your trials, troubles, and afflictions, and you will be lifted up at the last day.

Now, my son, I do not want you to think I know these things on my own. It is the Spirit of God within me that makes these things known to me. For if I had not been born of God, I would not have known these things.

But the Lord in his great mercy sent his angel to tell me that I must stop the work of destruction among his people. I have seen an angel face to face. He spoke to me, and his voice was like thunder and shook the whole earth.

And I spent three days and three nights in the most bitter pain and anguish of soul. Not until I cried out to the Lord Jesus Christ for mercy did I receive forgiveness of my sins. But I did cry out to him, and I did find peace for my soul.

And now, my son, I have told you this so you may learn wisdom—so you may learn from me that there is no other way for a person to be saved except in and through Christ. He is the life and the light of the world. He is the word of truth and righteousness.
And now, since you have begun to teach the word, I want you to continue teaching. And I want you to be disciplined and moderate in all things.
Make sure you are not lifted up in pride. Do not boast about your own wisdom or your great strength.
Be bold, but not pushy. Also make sure you control all your passions so you may be filled with love. And avoid laziness.
Do not pray like the Zoramites do, for you have seen that they pray to be noticed by others and praised for their wisdom.

Do not say: O God, I thank thee that we are better than our brethren. Instead, say: O Lord, forgive my unworthiness, and remember my brethren in mercy. Yes, acknowledge your unworthiness before God at all times.
And may the Lord bless your soul and receive you at the last day into his kingdom, to sit down in peace. Now go, my son, and teach the word to this people. Be serious about it. My son, farewell.

39.text: And now, my son, I have more to say to you than what I said to your brother. Have you not noticed how steady your brother has been—his faithfulness and his dedication to keeping the commandments of God? Has he not set a good example for you?
You did not pay as much attention to my words as your brother did among the Zoramites. Now this is what I hold against you: you went around boasting about your strength and your wisdom.
And that is not all, my son. You did something deeply painful to me: you abandoned the ministry and went to the land of Siron, near the borders of the Lamanites, to pursue the harlot Isabel.
Yes, she led many hearts astray, but that was no excuse for you, my son. You should have attended to the ministry you were given.
Do you not know, my son, that these things are an abomination in the sight of the Lord—the most abominable of all sins, except for the shedding of innocent blood or denying the Holy Ghost?

For if you deny the Holy Ghost after it has dwelled in you, and you know that you are denying it, that is an unpardonable sin. And whoever murders against the light and knowledge of God, it is not easy for that person to receive forgiveness. I say to you, my son, it is not easy for that person to receive forgiveness.
And now, my son, I wish to God that you had not been guilty of such a serious sin. I would not bring up your crimes and trouble your soul if it were not for your own good.
But you cannot hide your sins from God. Unless you repent, they will stand as a witness against you at the last day.
